title: crusea glabra
intro: Crusea glabra, also known as Mountain Mint, is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of North America, characterized by its square stems and small, white flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in treating wounds, burns, and skin irritations. The health benefits of Crusea glabra include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, which can help combat infections and promote overall well-being. Herbal preparations of Crusea glabra include teas, infusions, and topical applications, which can be used to soothe sore muscles, calm digestive issues, and provide relief from respiratory problems.
